title: The 2017 Paradigm Shift
concept: The landmark 2017 paper proved that recurrence can be discarded entirely, replacing RNNs with pure self-attention layers for state-of-the-art vision/text models.

exercises.prompt: What is the name of the famous 2017 paper that changed how AI models process language?
exercises.explain: The landmark 2017 paper is famously titled 'Attention Is All You Need', signaling that attention could replace older recurrent networks.

cards.title: The Memory Bottleneck
cards.body: Imagine trying to read a book, but you can only see one word at a time, and you forget the beginning of the sentence by the time you reach the end. That is how computers used to read before a massive breakthrough in 2017 changed everything!

cards.title: Attention Is All You Need
cards.body: In 2017, researchers published a paper titled "Attention Is All You Need." It introduced the Transformer, a new architecture that completely removed recurrence (processing step-by-step) and relied entirely on self-attention to process whole sequences at once.

cards.title: The Google Doc Analogy
cards.body: Think of a school project. The old way (RNNs) was like a game of 'Telephone' where students passed a notebook down a long line, each adding one sentence. By the time it reached the end, the early details were lost. The 2017 way is like a shared Google Doc where all students read and write at the same time, seeing every connection instantly!

cards.title: Translation Speedrun
cards.body: Before 2017, translating a long paragraph took time because the AI read word 1, then word 2, then word 3. Today, modern translation tools look at the entire paragraph instantly, understanding how 'bank' at the end relates to 'river' at the beginning.

cards.title: Myth: Order is Essential
cards.body: Many believe that AI models must process language in the order it is written to understand it. In reality, the 2017 shift proved that analyzing all words at the same time (in parallel) works much better and is incredibly fast.

cards.title: Chain vs. Web
cards.body: See how the core way of processing information shifted from sequential to parallel connections.

right.text: Processes all items at once. Direct attention connections exist between every single word instantly.

left.text: Processes step-by-step. Information must pass through every link, risking loss of early memory.

exercises.prompt: What was the main limitation of older RNN models before the 2017 paradigm shift?

options.text: They processed words in reverse alphabetical order.
They had to process text step-by-step, making them slow and forgetful.
They did not require any computer memory to run.
They were much faster than modern Transformers.

exercises.explain: Before 2017, Recurrent Neural Networks (RNNs) processed text step-by-step. The 2017 shift allowed models to process the entire sequence at the same time (in parallel).

exercises.prompt: True or False: The self-attention architecture introduced in 2017 is used for both language and computer vision tasks.
exercises.explain: The 2017 paper proved that pure self-attention is highly effective for both vision (images) and text models.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the missing word to complete the description of the 2017 AI breakthrough.
exercises.sentence: The landmark 2017 paper showed we could completely discard [blank] and use self-attention instead.
exercises.explain: The breakthrough in 2017 proved that we could throw away recurrence (step-by-step processing) and rely entirely on self-attention.

exercises.prompt: Which of these descriptions represents the OLD way of processing text (pre-2017) instead of the modern Transformer approach?

options.text: Our model utilizes pure self-attention mechanism, entirely discarding recurrent layers to process the document in parallel.
By replacing the recurrent architecture with transformer blocks, we achieved state-of-the-art results on text translation.
We built a system that reads each word one by one, keeping track of history in a loop before moving to the next word.

exercises.explain: The third option is generated by a human because it describes the slow, step-by-step processing of traditional RNNs, rather than the parallel self-attention introduced in 2017.

exercises.prompt: What major structural change did the landmark 2017 paper introduce to AI models?

options.text: It made models process text slower by adding more recurrent loops.
It discarded recurrence entirely, replacing it with parallel self-attention.
It proved that computers can only understand text one word at a time.
It replaced attention layers with traditional database searches.

exercises.explain: The 2017 paradigm shift discarded recurrence (processing step-by-step in order) and replaced it entirely with self-attention, allowing computers to process all words simultaneously.
